mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns exceed 0.5% in > 70% of months where CPI inflation surpasses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a negative information coefficient over consecutive 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following a 1.0% monthly drop in industrial production.
